raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section’s definitions and contribution framing may need reframing, especially around interpretation alignment and user intent.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the argument coherently connects ambiguous queries, benchmark labels, accuracy, and alignment claims.","R1.3":"Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing because the section claims user-intent alignment from majority-vote accuracy results.","R1.4":"The explicit first-GNN and extension claims require assessing novelty relative to the named nearby works.","R1.5":"Because the request targets an entire section, section-level narrative order and placement of motivation, evaluation, results, and contribution are central.","R1.6":"General help with a section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended and about facts not verifiable from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The section contains potentially strong alignment and novelty claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within projection rather than editing, critiquing, or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should flag responsibilities that reduce misleading research claims or downstream misuse of unsupported alignment language.","RX.5":"The responsibility assignment should be traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The section’s definitions and contribution framing may need reframing, especially around interpretation alignment and user intent.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the argument coherently connects ambiguous queries, benchmark labels, accuracy, and alignment claims.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing because the section claims user-intent alignment from majority-vote accuracy results.
rationale.R1.4: The explicit first-GNN and extension claims require assessing novelty relative to the named nearby works.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets an entire section, section-level narrative order and placement of motivation, evaluation, results, and contribution are central.
rationale.R1.6: General help with a section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended and about facts not verifiable from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains potentially strong alignment and novelty claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection rather than editing, critiquing, or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should flag responsibilities that reduce misleading research claims or downstream misuse of unsupported alignment language.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility assignment should be traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact.
